description:
# Stretch fixture: tagged-tuple AST evaluator

## What

Add `fixtures/ast_eval/` — a multi-clause fn that pattern-matches
on tagged tuples `{:num, n}`, `{:add, a, b}`, `{:mul, a, b}` and
recursively evaluates the expression to an int. Classic Erlang/Elixir
"eval" teaching shape; first fixture to exercise tagged-tuple
dispatch end-to-end.

```fz
fn eval({:num, n}), do: n
fn eval({:add, a, b}), do: eval(a) + eval(b)
fn eval({:mul, a, b}), do: eval(a) * eval(b)

fn main() do
  print(eval({:num, 42}))
  print(eval({:add, {:num, 2}, {:mul, {:num, 3}, {:num, 4}}}))
end
```

Expected stdout: `42\n14\n`.

## Why

Surveying the existing fixture set: tail recursion, list primitives,
HOFs, mutual recursion, multi-process relay are all covered. The gap
is pattern-matching on user-shaped compound data — every existing
fixture matches on scalars (ints/atoms/bools) or list cells. This
fixture closes that gap with the smallest classic Erlang program
that exercises it.

## Blocked by

- fz-ul4.35 (interp MakeTuple + TupleField)
- fz-ul4.36 (TypeTest reads descr.tuples)

Without those, JIT silently halts and interp errors with
"not yet supported".

## Paths

`[jit, interp, aot]` minimum. Try `repl` last — if it lands
cleanly, add it; if it doesn't, file the gap as its own ticket
rather than dropping the path silently.

## Acceptance

- `fixtures/ast_eval/input.fz` + README.md with frontmatter (purpose,
  paths) + expected.txt + blessed expected.specs + expected.clif.
- `cargo test fixture_matrix` passes the new trials.
- `cargo test fixture_index_up_to_date` regenerates with the new
  row included.
- All existing fixtures still pass.
title: fixture: ast_eval — Erlang-style expression evaluator
